title: Recover prior session context during resume across manual Zed session changes
summary: Ensure lifecycle-aware resume behavior can recover the active transaction's prior `session_id` and related context from canonical persisted state when the user starts a new Zed session and the old session id is no longer available from live caller context. This ticket covers canonical lookup order, machine-readable recovery failures, and regression protection for manual session-switch resume flows without weakening strict lifecycle validation.
outputs: Resume-safe session-context recovery rules for lifecycle-aware tools
Implementation support for recovering prior `session_id` from canonical persisted state
Structured machine-readable failure behavior when safe session-context recovery is not possible
Regression tests covering manual Zed session-switch resume flows
acceptance_criteria: Resume-capable lifecycle tools do not assume the caller still has the original session id in memory after a manual Zed session switch.
When a non-terminal active transaction exists, prior session context is recovered from materialized `tx_state` when available.
When materialized state is missing or incomplete, canonical event history or replay-derived state is used as the fallback source for prior session context.
If safe session-context recovery is not possible, the tool returns a structured machine-readable failure instead of only a free-form validation string.
Recovery behavior preserves strict canonical lifecycle validation and does not guess healthy state from ambiguous or invalid history.
Regression tests cover both successful prior-session recovery and irrecoverable session-context failure paths.
tasks: Audit lifecycle-aware wrapper and helper entry points that currently require explicit `session_id` input during resume flows.

Define the canonical lookup order for prior session context during resume: materialized `tx_state`, then canonical `tx_event_log` or replay-derived state, then structured recovery failure if neither source is sufficient.
Implement or refactor shared resume-context helpers so lifecycle-aware tools can recover prior `session_id` and related transaction identity safely from canonical persisted state.
Ensure recovered session context is derived from canonical persisted state rather than caller memory or ad hoc inference.
Add structured failure behavior for missing, incomplete, ambiguous, or unrecoverable prior session context during resume.
Add regression tests for manual Zed session changes where a new session resumes prior non-terminal work without the old session id in live caller context.
notes: This ticket exists because Zed session switching is user-driven and the runtime cannot rely on a reliable session-close signal from the client.
The main recovery source should be canonical persisted state, not transient agent memory.
Prefer materialized `tx_state` first, but use canonical event history or replay-derived state as the fallback when materialized state is missing or incomplete.
Structured recovery failures should make it clear whether the problem is missing context, ambiguous canonical state, or integrity-blocked history.
This ticket should improve resumability without weakening strict lifecycle or integrity guarantees.
